# polymarket_order_books (Data Source)

Fetches the live order books for several CLOB outcome tokens in a single request.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "polymarket_market" "m" { id = "540817" }

# Fetch the order book for every outcome of the market in one request.
data "polymarket_order_books" "all" {
  token_ids = data.polymarket_market.m.clob_token_ids
}

output "best_asks" {
  value = [for b in data.polymarket_order_books.all.books : try(b.asks[0].price, null)]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `token_ids` (List of String) CLOB ERC-1155 token IDs to fetch, from markets' `clob_token_ids`.

### Read-Only

- `books` (Attributes List) Order books, one per requested token. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--books))

<a id="nestedatt--books"></a>
### Nested Schema for `books`

Read-Only:

- `asks` (Attributes List) Sell orders, descending by price. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--books--asks))
- `bids` (Attributes List) Buy orders, ascending by price. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--books--bids))
- `hash` (String) Content hash of the snapshot.
- `last_trade_price` (String) Most recent trade price for this token, in `[0, 1]`.
- `market` (String) Condition ID of the parent market.
- `min_order_size` (String) Minimum order size accepted for this token, in shares.
- `neg_risk` (Boolean) Whether the token is in a negative-risk market.
- `tick_size` (String) Smallest price increment accepted for this token.
- `timestamp` (String) Server snapshot time, in milliseconds since the Unix epoch.
- `token_id` (String) Token this book is for (its asset ID).

<a id="nestedatt--books--asks"></a>
### Nested Schema for `books.asks`

Read-Only:

- `price` (String) Price of this level, in `[0, 1]`, as a decimal string.
- `size` (String) Total size resting at this price level, in shares.


<a id="nestedatt--books--bids"></a>
### Nested Schema for `books.bids`

Read-Only:

- `price` (String) Price of this level, in `[0, 1]`, as a decimal string.
- `size` (String) Total size resting at this price level, in shares.
